chunks.text: EECS Undergraduate Newsletter | February 14, 2025. This newsletter provides updates for EECS undergraduate students at UC Berkeley. It includes important announcements, upcoming events, and opportunities relevant to students in electrical engineering and computer sciences. The newsletter aims to keep students informed about academic deadlines, research opportunities, and campus resources. Students are encouraged to regularly check for new information that may affect their academic planning and career development.

The newsletter highlights key dates and deadlines for the current semester. Students should note registration periods, add/drop deadlines, and exam schedules. It also mentions workshops on resume building and interview preparation offered by the career center. Additionally, there are reminders about applying for scholarships and research assistant positions. Staying updated with these deadlines helps students manage their academic responsibilities effectively.

Upcoming events include guest lectures from industry professionals and alumni panels. These events provide networking opportunities and insights into various career paths in technology. Students can learn about internships and job openings at leading companies. The newsletter also lists social events organized by student clubs to foster community among EECS majors. Participating in these activities can enhance both professional and personal development.

Opportunities featured include research projects with faculty members and summer internship programs. Students are encouraged to apply early as positions are competitive. The newsletter provides links to application portals and contact information for inquiries. It also mentions study abroad programs and exchange opportunities for those interested in international experiences. Exploring these options can broaden students' academic and cultural perspectives.

Resources available to students include tutoring services, academic advising, and mental health support. The newsletter directs students to online portals for scheduling appointments. It also lists useful websites for course materials and technical support. Students are reminded to utilize these resources to succeed in their studies and maintain well-being. Accessing support early can help address challenges proactively.
